compiled_state.author: RebarBotNava
compiled_state.description:
## What

Generate `docs/cli-reference.md` from the CLI's OWN help data, so the command reference
cannot drift, and gate it in CI like `scripts/gen_env_registry.py`.

## Source of truth (verified — the CLI surface is two disjoint families)
1. **50 help-backed subcommands** — fully enumerable, RELIABLE help text:
   - `rebar._cli._help.known_subcommands() -> frozenset[str]` (returns 50).
   - `rebar._cli._help.subcommand_help(sub) -> str|None` — the full pinned usage text
     (package data under `rebar._cli.help/*.txt`). Reliable; use this for full help.
2. **16 intercept-arm commands** — handled in `src/rebar/_cli/__init__.py` main() before the
   `known_subcommands()` check (~lines 372-447), with NO `help/*.txt` and no overview row:
   `reconcile, review, review-code, scan-spec, verify-completion, review-plan, sign-review,
   enrich, explain, verify-commit-ticket, workflow, llm, jira-onboard, prompt, criteria,
   config`.

**Do NOT subprocess-capture `rebar <cmd> --help` for the intercepts.** Verified gotcha:
`rebar enrich --help` has no argparse parser (`cmd_enrich()` in `rebar/llm/enrich_drain.py`)
— it calls `drain()` and prints JSON with exit 0, so a "non-empty + exit 0" guard would
silently store JSON garbage as "help". Subprocess capture across 16 heterogeneous commands
is fragile. Instead: carry a hardcoded **curated one-line description** for each of the 16
intercept commands in the generator (`INTERCEPT_COMMANDS: dict[str,str]`), and DRIFT-GATE
the dict's key set against the `if argv[0] == "..."` ladder in `_cli/__init__.py` so a
newly-added intercept without a curated entry fails the generator loudly.

Total ~66 commands, all listed; the 50 with full pinned help, the 16 with a curated
one-liner (marked as advanced/intercept commands, "see `rebar <cmd> --help`").

## Scope
- Add `scripts/gen_cli_reference.py` (new): Family 1 via `_help.known_subcommands()` +
  `subcommand_help()`; Family 2 via the `INTERCEPT_COMMANDS` curated dict. Emit
  `docs/cli-reference.md` with a "Generated by … do not edit" header (mirror
  `gen_env_registry.py`), each command name rendered backtick-wrapped for exact matching.
- The generator asserts `set(INTERCEPT_COMMANDS) == <ladder set parsed from _cli/__init__.py>`
  and exits non-zero on mismatch (so a new/removed intercept is caught — no subprocess).
- `--check` mode: regenerate in memory, exit non-zero if committed file differs.
- Wire a drift gate into BOTH `.github/workflows/test.yml` AND
  `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml` (the actual Verified gate; a parity test
  `tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py` ENFORCES every `scripts/*.py` in test.yml also
  appears in gerrit-verify.yaml — omitting the latter breaks `make test`).

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] `docs/cli-reference.md` is generated (marked generated) and lists BOTH families:
      every `known_subcommands()` entry (50) with its full help AND all 16 intercept
      commands with a curated one-liner.
- [ ] Completeness proof, EXACT backtick match (no substring false positives like
      `review` vs `review-plan`), prints nothing:
      `python scripts/gen_cli_reference.py && for c in $(python -c "from rebar._cli import _help; print(' '.join(sorted(_help.known_subcommands())))") reconcile review review-code scan-spec verify-completion review-plan sign-review enrich explain verify-commit-ticket workflow llm jira-onboard prompt criteria config; do grep -qF "\`$c\`" docs/cli-reference.md || echo "MISSING $c"; done`
- [ ] Intercept-drift guard: `set(INTERCEPT_COMMANDS)` equals the ladder set parsed from
      `_cli/__init__.py`; a mismatch makes the generator exit non-zero. DETERMINISTIC proof
      (prints `CAUGHT`): temporarily remove one key from the dict in memory and confirm the
      generator's self-check fails — covered by a held-out unit test that mutates the dict
      and asserts a non-zero/raise. (The generator runs this parity check on every run.)
- [ ] Drift gate works — a stale committed reference fails `--check` (prints `CAUGHT`):
      `python scripts/gen_cli_reference.py; printf '\nstale\n' >> docs/cli-reference.md; python scripts/gen_cli_reference.py --check >/dev/null 2>&1 && echo MISS || echo CAUGHT; python scripts/gen_cli_reference.py`
- [ ] The gate is wired in BOTH CI files (prints nothing):
      `for f in .github/workflows/test.yml .github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml; do grep -qF 'gen_cli_reference.py' "$f" || echo "NOT WIRED: $f"; done`
      and `python -m pytest tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py -q` passes.
- [ ] `make lint && make typecheck && make test` stay green.

Files: `scripts/gen_cli_reference.py` (new), `docs/cli-reference.md` (new/generated),
`.github/workflows/test.yml`, `.github/workflows/gerrit-verify.yaml`.

file_impact.reason: new CLI-reference generator (50 help-backed + 16 curated intercepts)

compiled_state.title: P1: Generate the CLI command reference (generator + drift gate)
